title: To Miss Atkinson, On The Extreme Diffidence Which She Displays To Strangers.
content:
Just as a fawn, in forest shade,
    Trembling to meet th' admiring eye,
    I've seen thee try to hide, sweet maid!
    Thy charms behind thy modesty.

    Thus too I've seen at midnight steal
    A fleecy cloud before the wind,
    And veil, tho' it could not conceal,
    The brilliant light that shone behind.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, John Carr (Sir) delivers a powerful performance in "To Miss Atkinson, On The Extreme Diffidence Which She Displays To Strangers."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
